purpose: Metamorphic-relation coverage ledger — the POSITIVE complement to docs/SOUNDNESS_INVARIANTS.json. A soundness probe forges a VIOLATING input and asserts rejection; a metamorphic probe asserts an ALGEBRAIC RELATION that must be preserved across a protocol transformation (e.g. adapt then extract recovers the witness; aggregating partial signatures equals a single-key signature; commitments are homomorphic). A roundtrip test proves one happy path; a metamorphic relation pins the STRUCTURE of the transform across the input family, catching structural breaks that a single honest roundtrip cannot. Enforced by ci/check_metamorphic_coverage.py.
methodology: For each transform F (and its inverse/companion G), state an identity that must hold for ALL valid inputs — not 'one sign/verify works' but 'extract(pre, adapt(pre,t)) == +/-t for every t', 'verify(adapt(pre,t))', 'commit(a)+commit(b)==commit(a+b)'. A bug that breaks the correspondence escapes roundtrip tests but breaks the relation.

relations.transform: adapt(pre, t) -> full sig ; extract(pre, full) -> witness
relations.relation: ecdsa_verify(m,P,adapt(pre,t)); extract(pre,adapt(pre,t)) == +/-t; adapt preserves r; pre-sig is not a valid plain sig; adapt is deterministic; distinct adaptors recover distinct witnesses (no crossover).
commit(v1,r1) + commit(v2,r2) == commit(v1+v2, r1+r2): the commitment map is a group homomorphism (the property confidential-transaction value balancing relies on).

relations.transform: aggregate(partial_sign_i for i in signers) -> BIP-340 sig under aggregate pubkey Q
relations.relation: An n-of-n MuSig2 aggregate signature verifies under Q exactly as a single BIP-340 signature would under a key whose secret is the (key-aggregation-weighted) sum of the signer secrets: schnorr_verify(Q_x, m, aggregate(...)). Multi-party signing is observationally equivalent to single-key signing on the aggregate key.

relations.transform: any t-of-n quorum -> group signature under group pubkey Y
relations.relation: Any authorized t-of-n quorum reconstructs a signature that verifies under the SAME group key Y, independent of which t signers participated: the Lagrange-weighted aggregate is invariant to the participating subset. schnorr_verify(Y_x, m, sig) holds for every valid quorum.
